description: Continue Orion Agent toward an OpenClaw-style stable runtime core by hardening recovery state flow, context budget governance, tool permission context, and structured recovery trace payloads without sacrificing the current visual web console experience.

userStories.title: Harden Recovery State Machine
userStories.description: As a runtime maintainer, I want failure classification and recovery transitions to be expressed through a more explicit state machine so skip, retry, partial replan, and resume behavior are predictable and traceable.
acceptanceCriteria: Recovery-related transitions are modeled through clearer state and decision boundaries rather than scattered helper behavior
Skip-step and rebuild-remaining-plan behavior remain compatible or become stricter in a documented way
Task records retain enough structured recovery metadata for current APIs and UI consumers
Add or update focused backend unit tests for recovery transitions and fallback paths
Run the relevant backend unittest modules

userStories.title: Strengthen Context Budget and Compression Governance
userStories.description: As a runtime maintainer, I want context layers to expose explicit budget usage, trimming reasons, and compression outputs so multi-turn sessions remain stable and debuggable.
acceptanceCriteria: Context building records clearer budget allocation, actual usage, and trimming or compression reasons per major layer
Multi-turn session summary and recent-message compression remain backward compatible with the current UI payload
At least the runtime path and context-related tests consume the structured budget or trimming metadata
Add or update backend unit tests for budget and compression metadata behavior
Run the relevant backend unittest modules

userStories.title: Unify ToolPool Permission Context
userStories.description: As a platform maintainer, I want tool execution to emit a stable permission context model so frontend flows, approvals, and audits can rely on one source of truth for risk and approval state.
acceptanceCriteria: Tool execution records expose a stable permission context structure covering risk level, approval requirement, approval status, and runtime enforcement result
Restricted and confirmable tools keep current behavior while emitting richer structured metadata
Frontend-facing task details remain compatible or improve without relying on guessed approval semantics
Add or update backend tests for permission context emission and approval-related tool invocation behavior
Run the relevant backend unittest modules and frontend build if touched

userStories.title: Standardize Recovery and Citation Trace Payloads
userStories.description: As a product maintainer, I want recovery traces and citation traces to share a more consistent structured payload so the console can explain both answer sources and recovery decisions with less UI-side inference.
acceptanceCriteria: Recovery traces expose structured fields for failure type, strategy, attempts, and resume origin
Citation and recovery payloads align on stable source or trace identifiers where appropriate
Existing console and task detail consumers remain compatible or become simpler
Add or update backend tests for trace payload generation and frontend build if touched
Run the matching backend unittest modules and frontend build if touched
